behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git ancestry/time evaluation plus PR-state join. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when repo-state and PR-state modules interact. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rebasing/ancestry and commit age are often computed via real git subprocess behavior; mocks can hide graph/time edge cases. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior has no AI-model decision or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, make main+feature history with controlled commit dates older/newer than threshold, run the actual staleness detector (including its git command path) with a test PR backend indicating no open PR, and assert stale=true only for branches meeting all three conditions.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is session bootstrap wiring plus detector output rendering at startup. MOCK-MISS: local function tests miss startup-path regressions. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this is tied to real session initialization/CLI process behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output governs correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the real session-start entrypoint in a temp repo configured as stale, capture startup output, and assert warning text includes actionable branch context (branch name, stale reason/details) exactly on stale branch and not otherwise.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function. MOCK-MISS: no multi-module handoff required. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP all absent. Rejected Integration+ because no OS/network/subprocess or external dependency is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive inputs of computed branch-state structs into the classifier function and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says against live repo and PR state. REAL-INFRA is required (real git state + real PR API/HTTP or equivalent live source). MOCK-HIDE: stubs can mask rendering differences caused by real state fetch/format boundaries. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI judgment in correctness criteria.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in an environment wired to real repository state and real PR state source, exercise one clean branch and one stale-no-PR branch, and assert rendered output shows the correct distinct statuses and stale marker text.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is state-handoff logic between staleness evaluator and PR-status interpretation (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr). Integration brake: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text does not require live API transport; PR-state fixture coverage can catch the core false-positive logic.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector + PR-state adapter in-process with fixture branches that otherwise look stale, feed PR states `open` and `merged`, and assert classifier/output never returns `stale-no-pr` for those cases.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real git history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry resolution after hash changes. MOCK-HIDE: fake commit graphs often miss subtle DAG/merge-base behavior. REAL-INFRA: requires executing actual git operations to reproduce. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model-dependent decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real git repo, create stale candidate branch, then perform real rebase and squash-merge scenarios that change commit hashes while integrating work; run detector and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale after integration.
